pi_items.source: Confirmed via setup_task.sh, export_result.sh, and setup_opentoonz.sh scripts.
Confirmed via setup_task.sh, export_result.sh, and verifier.py checks.
Confirmed via setup_task.sh, export_result.sh, and verifier.py checks.

Downloaded the actual OpenToonz sample data (https://github.com/opentoonz/opentoonz_sample/archive/refs/heads/master.zip) as dictated by setup_opentoonz.sh. Analyzing dwanko_run.tnz reveals 11 distinct columns in the Xsheet (10 levelColumns and 1 zeraryFxColumn), explicitly contradicting the author's assumption of exactly 3.
Confirmed via verifier.py which explicitly checks 'col_count <= 2' to award bonus feedback for a cleaner timeline.

privileged_info_summary: The task relies on the real OpenToonz dwanko_run.tnz sample downloaded from the official GitHub repository. While the target paths correctly match the verification scripts, a critical discrepancy exists in the original scene data: the actual dwanko_run.tnz contains 11 timeline columns, completely contradicting the prompt's assumption of 3 columns ('Back, Table, Char'). Consequently, instructing the agent to 'collapse Column 1 and 2' will leave 10 columns, fundamentally violating the instruction that 'The main timeline should only show the character animation and the collapsed background group' unless the agent autonomously deletes or hides the remaining 8 columns. Additionally, the original dwanko_run.tnz file contains malformed XML (an unescaped '&' in the Compression Type property on line 75), which perpetually causes python's xml.etree.ElementTree to throw a ParseError. If the agent's saved output preserves this defect, the verifier will critically fail to parse the scene structure, falsely depriving the agent of 40 points.
